output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 12/12.
Batch 1: Completed batch 1 tasks T1-T4. Resize and external-drop polling guards are in place, asset-drop track creation now re-checks latest state before creating a track, the undo/save race audit confirmed the existing skipHistory behavior is safe, and the batch checkpoint was written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_1.json`. Verification: `cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` passed with 23 test files and 79 tests.
Batch 2: T5 completed. Ran `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/`. Result: 23 test files passed and 79 tests passed. The plan’s 71-test expectation is stale relative to the current repository state, but the full current video-editor module passed. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Completed batch 3. `SortableRow`, `ClipAction`, and `AudioTrack` are memoized, `TimelineCanvas` now passes row-scoped resize-override subsets instead of the whole override map, and `ClipAction`'s context-menu handler now reads volatile shot-action state from refs so the memo boundary can hold. Verification: `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` passed with 23 test files and 82 tests. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Completed batch 4 as a no-op assessment. The T6 structure is sufficient: `resizeOverrides` stays local to `TimelineCanvas`, `SortableRow` is memoized, and each row receives only its own resize-override subset, so only the actively resized row should rerender during preview. No ref-plus-forceUpdate path was needed. Verification: `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` passed with 23 test files and 82 tests. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Batch 5 completed. Ran the full `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest module after the Phase 2 rendering fixes. Result: 23 test files passed and 82 tests passed. The plan’s 71-test expectation is stale relative to the current repository state, but the full current video-editor module passed. Existing checkpoint at `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_5.json` already matched this result, so no file update was needed.
Batch 6: Completed batch 6. Extracted selection state and synchronization from `useTimelineState` into a new standalone `useTimelineSelection` hook, and rewired `useTimelineState` to consume it. Verification: `useTimelineState.ts` is now 397 lines, down from 523, and the full video-editor Vitest module passed with 23 test files and 82 tests. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_6.json`.
Batch 7: Completed batch 7. Added a typed `TimelineEventBus` and moved the cross-hook coordination off mutable refs: commit now emits `beforeCommit`/`pruneSelection`/`scheduleSave`, persistence emits `saveSuccess`, `useTimelineSave` owns the bus and subscribes save scheduling, and `useTimelineState` subscribes history, render-dirty, and selection-prune listeners. Verification: grep confirms `onSaveSuccessRef`, `onBeforeCommitRef`, `pruneSelectionRef`, and `scheduleSaveRef` are gone from the hooks layer, and the full video-editor Vitest module passed with 23 test files and 82 tests. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_7.json`.
Batch 8: Completed batch 8. Eliminated the timeline data adapter shape from the remaining hooks and `useTimelineState`, so the editor state now passes explicit values and typed operations instead of rebuilding a `UseTimelineDataResult` object. Verification: `UseTimelineDataResult`, `dataHook.*`, `applyTimelineEdit`, and `applyResolvedConfigEdit` are gone from `src/tools/video-editor/`, and the full video-editor Vitest module passed with 23 test files and 82 tests. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_8.json`.
Batch 9: Completed batch 9. Split the editor context into `TimelineEditorDataContext` and `TimelineEditorOpsContext`, added `useTimelineEditorData()` and `useTimelineEditorOps()`, kept `useTimelineEditorContext()` as a deprecated merged compatibility hook, and updated `VideoEditorProvider` to nest the two editor sub-providers ahead of the existing chrome/playback providers. Verification: grep confirms the two new sub-context hooks/providers and the deprecated merged hook markers, and the full video-editor Vitest module passed with 24 test files and 88 tests. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_9.json`.
Batch 10: Completed batch 10. Migrated the seven planned editor-context consumers from the deprecated merged hook to `useTimelineEditorData()` and/or `useTimelineEditorOps()`: `TimelineEditor`, `PropertiesPanel`, `PreviewPanel`, `AssetPanel`, `CompactPreview`, `useSelectedMediaClips`, and `VideoEditorShell`. During verification, fixed a small helper regression in `TimelineEditor` so `resolveSelectedGenerationIdsForShotCreation` accepts the existing test `registry` shape as well as the optimized asset-generation map path used by the component. Verification: grep shows none of the seven target consumers still import `useTimelineEditorContext`, and the full video-editor Vitest module passed with 24 test files and 89 tests. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_10.json`.
Batch 11: Completed batch 11. Extracted the editor/chrome/playback context assembly out of `useTimelineState` into a dedicated helper module, which reduced `useTimelineState.ts` to 268 lines and left it reading as hook composition plus event wiring. Audited `useDerivedTimeline.ts`: it remains unchanged at 89 lines, uses pure `useMemo` chains, and has no mutable ref cache usage. Verification passed with the full video-editor Vitest module: 24 test files and 89 tests. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_11.json`.
Batch 12: Completed batch 12. Added regression coverage for resize pending-op lifecycle, per-upload external-drop pending-op tracking, timeline event-bus subscription cleanup, and the editor data/ops provider split. Also ran a temporary one-file Vitest repro for the cancelled-resize pending-op bug, confirmed it passed, and deleted the repro file. Final verification passed with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/`: 26 test files and 94 tests passed. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/take-the-video-editor-20260406-0351/execution_batch_12.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added resize pending-op lifecycle in TimelineCanvas: increment after `onActionResizeStart` and decrement before the cancelled check in `endResize`, so both normal completion and pointer-cancel paths release the polling block. Verified in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` and by the full video-editor vitest run passing.
commands_run: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

task_updates.executor_notes: Threaded `pendingOpsRef` through `useExternalDrop` and `useTimelineState`, then moved upload blocking to per-file scope: each async upload increments inside the file loop before its IIFE and decrements in that IIFE's `finally` block. Updated hook test call sites and verified with the full video-editor vitest run passing.
commands_run: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

task_updates.executor_notes: Audited `handleAssetDrop` and added an explicit synchronous re-check of `dataRef.current` immediately before creating a track. If another completed drop already materialized a compatible track, the later call now reuses it instead of creating a duplicate; otherwise it creates the track against the latest state. Verified in `useAssetManagement.ts` and by the full video-editor vitest run passing.
commands_run: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

task_updates.executor_notes: Audit confirmed no undo/redo vs save-history race. `useTimelineCommit` only invokes `onBeforeCommit` when `shouldSave && !skipHistory`. Save-success reconciliation in `useTimelinePersistence` commits with `save:false` and `skipHistory:true`. Undo/redo `restoreSnapshot` commits with `save:true` but `skipHistory:true`. `useTimelineState` wires `history.onBeforeCommit` into `save.onBeforeCommitRef`, so `skipHistory` prevents history re-entry during save reconciliation or undo/redo. No code changes were needed for this audit conclusion.
commands_run: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

The plan’s 71-test expectation is stale relative to the current repo state, but all current video-editor tests passed.
commands_run: cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

task_updates.executor_notes: Assessed the resize-preview rerender path after T6 and kept it as a no-op. `resizeOverrides` remains local to `TimelineCanvas`, the memoized `SortableRow` receives only row-scoped override subsets, and the row render path uses a shared empty override object for unaffected rows; combined with `TimelineEditor` owning `getActionRender` outside that local resize state, this is sufficient for only the actively resized row to rerender during preview. No ref+forceUpdate path was needed. Verified by code inspection in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` lines 284, 321-339, and 739-747 plus `TimelineEditor.tsx` lines 329-390, and by the full video-editor Vitest module passing (23 files / 82 tests).

task_updates.executor_notes: Extracted timeline selection state and synchronization into a standalone `useTimelineSelection` hook with an explicit `{ dataHook, clearGallerySelection, registerPeerClear }` interface. The hook now owns multi-select composition, clip-selection adapters, gallery peer-clear registration, prune-selection ref wiring, and the selection-scoped derived timeline. `useTimelineState.ts` dropped from 523 lines to 397 lines (126-line reduction), leaving the hook substantially slimmer and easier to read as composition glue. Verified by running the full `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest module, which passed with 23 test files / 82 tests.
commands_run: cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

task_updates.executor_notes: Added a typed `TimelineEventBus` and migrated the four cross-hook ref bridges to event emission/subscription. `useTimelineCommit` now emits `beforeCommit`, `pruneSelection`, and `scheduleSave`; `useTimelinePersistence` emits `saveSuccess`; `useTimelineSave` owns the bus and subscribes `scheduleSave`; `useTimelineState` subscribes history, render-dirty, and selection-prune listeners from the bus. The old `onSaveSuccessRef`, `onBeforeCommitRef`, `pruneSelectionRef`, and `scheduleSaveRef` symbols are gone from the hooks layer, and the former wiring `useLayoutEffect` blocks were removed from `useTimelineState`, `useTimelineSave`, and the selection prune bridge. Verified by grep checks and by the full `src/tools/video-editor/` Vitest module passing with 23 test files / 82 tests.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. `endResize` now decrements `pendingOpsRef.current` before the cancelled gate, so both cancelled and successful resize endings release the pending-op block.
Confirmed. `pendingOpsRef` increments once per file inside the `for` loop and decrements in the corresponding upload IIFE `finally` block, not once for the whole drop.

Confirmed. `handleAssetDrop` now re-reads `dataRef.current` immediately before track creation and reuses an already-created compatible track if one exists, preventing duplicate track creation across concurrent async completions.

Confirmed. `commitData` skips `onBeforeCommit` whenever `skipHistory:true` is set, and both save reconciliation and undo/redo restoration use `skipHistory:true`. This was audit-only; no code changes were made for T4.
Yes. The full post-Phase-1 video-editor suite passed. The repository currently has 23 passing test files / 79 passing tests, so the plan’s 71-test count is outdated but fully covered by this run.
Confirmed. `SortableRow` is memoized, and each row now receives only its own resize-override subset instead of the full override map.
Confirmed. `ClipAction` is memoized, and the context-menu snapshot/open logic now reads volatile shot-action values from refs so the menu handler stays stable.
Confirmed. `AudioTrack` is memoized and the composite key for Remotion remount behavior was left intact.
Assessed and documented as a no-op. The T6 structure is sufficient: only the active row receives a changing resize-override subset, so a ref+forceUpdate path was not needed.
Yes. The full post-Phase-2 video-editor suite passed. The repository currently has 23 passing test files / 82 passing tests, so the plan’s 71-test count is outdated but fully covered by this run.
Yes. `useTimelineSelection` now exists as a standalone hook with a clear explicit interface, and `useTimelineState.ts` was reduced from 523 lines to 397 lines by moving the selection slice out.

Yes. All four ref bridges were replaced by event-bus emissions/subscriptions, and the old wiring `useLayoutEffect` blocks were removed from `useTimelineState`, `useTimelineSave`, and the selection prune bridge.
Yes. `UseTimelineDataResult` is gone, the old adapter/object reassembly is gone, and the downstream hooks now consume specific typed args instead of the adapter shape.
Yes. There are exactly two new editor sub-contexts, `useTimelineEditorData()` and `useTimelineEditorOps()` were added, and the merged `useTimelineEditorContext()` is explicitly marked deprecated.
Yes. All seven planned consumers now use the data/ops sub-context hooks instead of the merged editor context hook.
Yes. `useTimelineState.ts` is now 268 lines and reads as a composition diagram, while `useDerivedTimeline.ts` remains a clean 89-line pure-`useMemo` hook with no mutable ref cache.

Yes. New regression coverage was added for resize pending-op lifecycle, external-drop per-upload pending-op tracking, event-bus subscription cleanup, and the editor data/ops context split, and the full video-editor suite passed with 26 test files / 94 tests.
